source_description: Upbeat instrumental music plays. Outside a brick building on a city street corner, a person rides a bicycle past.
Upbeat instrumental music plays. Outside a brick building on a city street corner, a person rides a bicycle past.
The setting changes to a city street outside a storefront. Monica and Phoebe walk along the sidewalk, leading Chandler, Ross, Joey, and Rachel, who walk with their hands covering their eyes.
The setting changes to a city street outside a storefront. Monica and Phoebe walk along the sidewalk, leading Chandler, Ross, Joey, and Rachel, who walk with their hands covering their eyes.
The group continues walking along the sidewalk.
Chandler, holding a hand over his eyes, says, "Our eyes are closed, and we're about to cross the street. Very good." Monica guides them toward a parked blue van.
Chandler, holding a hand over his eyes, says, "Our eyes are closed, and we're about to cross the street. Very good." Monica guides them toward a parked blue van.
Phoebe says, "Okay, open up," gesturing with her hands. The group uncovers their eyes.
Phoebe says, "Okay, open up," gesturing with her hands. The group uncovers their eyes.
Chandler and Ross lower their hands. A large painting of a woman riding a dragon and holding a sword covers the side of the blue van. Ross gestures toward it with both hands.
Chandler and Ross lower their hands. A large painting of a woman riding a dragon and holding a sword covers the side of the blue van. Ross gestures toward it with both hands.
Ross says, "What do you want to show us? Because all I can see is this bitching van," gesturing toward the van with both hands.
Ross says, "What do you want to show us? Because all I can see is this bitching van," gesturing toward the van with both hands.
Ross says, "What do you want to show us? Because all I can see is this bitching van," gesturing toward the van with both hands.
Ross says, "What do you want to show us? Because all I can see is this bitching van," gesturing toward the van with both hands.
Audience laughter is heard. Phoebe points at the van.
Phoebe says, "Yeah, it's arcane business," smiling and gesturing.
Audience laughter is heard. Joey points at the van and says, "I think I know that girl." Rachel stands beside him with her arms crossed.
Audience laughter is heard. Joey points at the van and says, "I think I know that girl." Rachel stands beside him with her arms crossed.
The painting on the van is shown again. Monica gestures toward it.
Monica says, "All right, we're not going to really keep it this way, though."
Monica says, "All right, we're not going to really keep it this way, though."
Phoebe steps forward, pointing at the painting, and says, "No, we're going to paint over the sword and replace it with a baguette."
Phoebe steps forward, pointing at the painting, and says, "No, we're going to paint over the sword and replace it with a baguette."
Audience laughter is heard. Joey and Rachel look at the van. Rachel keeps her arms crossed.
Audience laughter is heard. Joey and Rachel look at the van. Rachel keeps her arms crossed.
Rachel says, "Oh, my God. I think I lost my virginity in that van."
Loud audience laughter is heard. Phoebe gestures toward the painting.
Phoebe points toward the front of the painting and says, "Also, we don't know what to do with this."
Phoebe points toward the front of the painting and says, "Also, we don't know what to do with this."
Two small red lights are embedded in the painted woman's chest on the van.
Two small red lights are embedded in the painted woman's chest on the van.
Joey points again and says, "Oh, yeah, I definitely know her."
Audience laughter is heard. The screen turns completely black.
Audience laughter is heard. The screen turns completely black.
Audience laughter is heard. The screen turns completely black.
The screen remains black.
The screen remains black.
The setting changes to the exterior of a brick apartment building at night, then changes to the inside of an apartment where Phoebe, Joey, Monica, and Ross are present.
The setting changes to the exterior of a brick apartment building at night, then changes to the inside of an apartment where Phoebe, Joey, Monica, and Ross are present.
Monica says, "Remember that guy from cooking school I told you about that put cilantro in everything?" standing in the kitchen area.
Monica says, "Remember that guy from cooking school I told you about that put cilantro in everything?" standing in the kitchen area.
Phoebe says, "Oh, sure. Cilantro Larry."
Phoebe says, "Oh, sure. Cilantro Larry."
Monica says, "Well, I'm going to fill in for him as food critic for The Chelsea Reporter." Ross steps forward and says, "Wow,"
Monica says, "Well, I'm going to fill in for him as food critic for The Chelsea Reporter." Ross steps forward and says, "Wow,"
Monica says, "Well, I'm going to fill in for him as food critic for The Chelsea Reporter." Ross steps forward and says, "Wow,"
Audience laughter is heard. Ross turns to Monica, holding a cereal box.
Ross says, "Monica, what an amazing opportunity to influence dozens of people," holding the box and gesturing with his free hand.
Ross says, "Monica, what an amazing opportunity to influence dozens of people," holding the box and gesturing with his free hand.
Ross says, "Monica, what an amazing opportunity to influence dozens of people," holding the box and gesturing with his free hand.
Monica looks at Ross with a tight-lipped expression. Phoebe turns to face Monica.
Monica looks at Ross with a tight-lipped expression. Phoebe turns to face Monica.
Phoebe asks, "How could you have said yes? What about our catering business?" Monica replies, "Oh, no, it was just one night a week," gesturing with her hands.
Phoebe asks, "How could you have said yes? What about our catering business?" Monica replies, "Oh, no, it was just one night a week," gesturing with her hands.
Phoebe asks, "How could you have said yes? What about our catering business?" Monica replies, "Oh, no, it was just one night a week," gesturing with her hands.
Monica continues, "Plus, I get to take all of you out for a lot of free dinners," pointing around the room.
Monica continues, "Plus, I get to take all of you out for a lot of free dinners," pointing around the room.
Joey says, "Well, in that case," looking toward Monica.
Joey says, "Well, in that case," looking toward Monica.

Phoebe smiles. Monica stands with her hands near her hips.
Phoebe says, "that was me hopping on board," making a small jumping motion.
Audience laughter is heard. Chandler enters the apartment through the front door, wearing a suit.
Chandler points toward the group and says, "Hey, guys. Hey, Ross."
Chandler points toward the group and says, "Hey, guys. Hey, Ross."
Chandler claps his hands together and points at Ross again. Ross sits on the arm of the sofa.
Chandler asks, "Are you ready to party?" pointing with both index fingers.
Ross looks up at Chandler.
Ross says, "I don't know. I could maybe go out for a couple of beers," gesturing with his hands.
Ross continues, "But there's this thing about bumblebees on the Discovery Channel," pointing his finger slightly.
Ross continues, "But there's this thing about bumblebees on the Discovery Channel," pointing his finger slightly.
Ross continues, "But there's this thing about bumblebees on the Discovery Channel," pointing his finger slightly.
Ross continues, "But there's this thing about bumblebees on the Discovery Channel," pointing his finger slightly.

Chandler says, "No, I don't think you heard me. Are you ready to party?" pointing both index fingers downward repeatedly.
Chandler says, "No, I don't think you heard me. Are you ready to party?" pointing both index fingers downward repeatedly.

Ross stares back, his mouth slightly open.

Chandler says, "Gandalf. Gandalf is coming to town. Kathy's with her parents. I have nothing to do."
Chandler says, "Gandalf. Gandalf is coming to town. Kathy's with her parents. I have nothing to do."

Ross says, "Dude, we are so gonna party," pointing at Chandler and gripping his hand.
Ross says, "Dude, we are so gonna party," pointing at Chandler and gripping his hand.

Phoebe holds a mug and says, "Okay, dude alert."

Chandler says, "I'm going to call and get off work tomorrow," stepping away. Ross adds, "I'm going to call..."
Chandler says, "I'm going to call and get off work tomorrow," stepping away. Ross adds, "I'm going to call..."

Chandler says, "This is going to be so cool. Dude, we never party anymore." Ross kneels next to the couch.
Chandler says, "This is going to be so cool. Dude, we never party anymore." Ross kneels next to the couch.
Ross and Chandler shake hands again. Ross makes a loud vocalization.
Ross and Chandler shake hands again. Ross makes a loud vocalization.
Monica says, "All right. Were you guys smoking something in the back of our van?" holding her spoon.
Monica says, "All right. Were you guys smoking something in the back of our van?" holding her spoon.
Audience laughter is heard. Ross sits down on the coffee table.
Joey asks, "Really? And what do you mean we never have fun anymore? You have fun with me."
Joey asks, "Really? And what do you mean we never have fun anymore? You have fun with me."
Joey continues, "Remember the time we saw those strippers and you paid me..." gesturing to himself.
Joey continues, "Remember the time we saw those strippers and you paid me..." gesturing to himself.

Audience laughter is heard. Chandler smiles.
Ross turns to Joey and says, "Joey, you are going to love this guy," gesturing with his hands.
Ross continues, "Gandalf is, like, the party wizard." Joey asks, "Why do you call him Gandalf?" leaning forward.
Ross continues, "Gandalf is, like, the party wizard." Joey asks, "Why do you call him Gandalf?" leaning forward.
Ross continues, "Gandalf is, like, the party wizard." Joey asks, "Why do you call him Gandalf?" leaning forward.

Ross raises his hands slightly and asks, "Hello. Didn't you read Lord of the Rings in high school?"
Chandler looks at Joey and smiles.
Joey replies, "No, I had sex in high school," leaning forward slightly.

In an office, Rachel holds a clipboard and approaches a desk where Joanna, a woman wearing a black jacket, sits looking down at an open file.
Rachel stops in front of the desk and says, "Joanna, I was wondering if I could ask you something. There's an opening for an assistant buyer in Junior Miss."
Rachel stops in front of the desk and says, "Joanna, I was wondering if I could ask you something. There's an opening for an assistant buyer in Junior Miss."
Rachel stops in front of the desk and says, "Joanna, I was wondering if I could ask you something. There's an opening for an assistant buyer in Junior Miss."
Joanna looks up from the file and interrupts, saying, "Okay, but that"
Joanna looks up from the file and interrupts, saying, "Okay, but that"
Joanna continues, "would actually be a big step down for me."
Rachel presses her hand to her chest and replies, "No, actually, I meant for me."
Joanna closes the folder on her desk.
Joanna picks up a pen and says, "The hiring committee is meeting people all day."
Joanna looks down at the desk briefly.
Looking back at Rachel, Joanna says, "Well, I wish I could say no, but I guess you can't stay my assistant forever."
Looking back at Rachel, Joanna says, "Well, I wish I could say no, but I guess you can't stay my assistant forever."
Looking back at Rachel, Joanna says, "Well, I wish I could say no, but I guess you can't stay my assistant forever."

Sophie turns and walks away. Joanna turns her attention back to Rachel.
Sophie turns and walks away. Joanna turns her attention back to Rachel.

Joanna stares at Rachel as Rachel clasps her hands tightly together.
